overview: Dimir Excruciator is a Combo-Control archetype that wins by reducing both players' libraries to six cards with Doomsday Excruciator, then milling the opponent to zero with Restless Reef. It supplements this primary combo with cheap disruption, Deceit copies via Superior Spider-Man, and flexible removal to handle aggressive and control strategies. The deck relies on early discard to enable faster combo turns and deep draw spells like Stock Up to find key pieces.
win_conditions: Execute Doomsday Excruciator to leave both players with six cards, then attack with Restless Reef to mill the opponent's remaining cards.
Copy Deceit with Superior Spider-Man to generate multiple 4/4 bodies and board/hand disruption.
Copy Doomsday Excruciator with Superior Spider-Man to accelerate the library reduction combo.

keep_criteria: Hands with early discard to put Doomsday Excruciator in the graveyard
Two-land hands with access to black mana for early interaction
Hands containing Deceit or Superior Spider-Man for early clock/disruption
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritizing fixing), 1-2 early interaction spells, and 1-2 draw engines.
Keep any hand with a turn-1 land and a cantrip or cheap interaction.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early discard or black mana sources
Hands with only high-cost sweepers or late-game threats
Hands missing lands or combo enablers
Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, 3+ lands and no spells, or only removal with no early play/draw.

examples.reason: Provides early discard to enable the combo, dual lands for mana, and a clock/disruption piece.

early_game: Play cheap disruption like Intimidation Tactics and Requiting Hex to slow aggressive decks.
Use Winternight Stories and Bitter Triumph to discard Doomsday Excruciator for future turns.
Establish a clock or board presence with Deceit.
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a cantrip or cheap interaction if available.
Turn 2: Develop board with a 2-drop threat or removal, ensuring mana base is online.
Prioritize fixing over raw speed to reliably cast U/B spells on curve.
Use early spells to probe for interaction and establish tempo.
mid_game: Execute the Doomsday Excruciator and Restless Reef combo to mill the opponent.
Use Stock Up to dig for combo pieces, lands, or sideboard cards while memorizing the bottom cards.
Copy Deceit or Excruciator with Superior Spider-Man to accelerate the win condition.
Preserve mana for interaction until threats are committed.
Use card draw to maintain hand size, but avoid overcommitting to one board state.
Manage graveyard by sideboarding in hate if needed, but don't over-index on it early.
Prioritize keeping interaction for the opponent's turn.
Commit threats only when you have removal or counterspells in hand to protect them.
Hold back on casting expensive finishers if the opponent has unanswered interaction.
late_game: Rely on Deceit's 4/4 body and ETB disruption as a recurring clock.
Use sweepers like Harvester of Misery and Deadly Cover-Up to clear boards or dig for answers.
Sideboard in Quantum Riddler for extra card draw and a resilient threat in attrition matchups.
High-impact threats or combo pieces that can close the game in 1-2 turns.
Use card advantage to ensure you have the final answer or the final blow.
Signature cards like Stock Up and Superior Spider-Man should be deployed to generate advantage or stabilize, not as early-game threats.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, board control, and the opponent has no clear path to stabilize.
Commit finishers when you can protect them or when the opponent is at lethal range.
Only commit if you have a plan to handle their response.

key_cards.name: Doomsday Excruciator
key_cards.notes: Exiles cards from both libraries until each has six, setting up the win condition.

key_cards.name: Restless Reef
key_cards.notes: Mills four cards from the opponent's deck upon attacking, leaving them with two turns to win.

key_cards.name: Superior Spider-Man
key_cards.notes: Copies Deceit for disruption/bodies or copies Excruciator to speed up the library reduction.

key_cards.name: Deceit
key_cards.notes: Functions as Distress and Into the Roil, providing a 4/4 body and ETB hand/board disruption.

key_cards.name: Stock Up
key_cards.notes: Dig Through Time-like draw that helps find combo pieces or lands; cards placed on bottom are drawn after Excruciator.

key_cards.name: Insatiable Avarice
key_cards.notes: Finds needed pieces and can force the opponent to draw, speeding up the win after Excruciator.

key_cards.name: Requiting Hex
key_cards.notes: Handles aggressive threats and counters mana explosions from cards like Badgermole Cub.

key_cards.name: Deadly Cover-Up
key_cards.notes: Clears boards and functions as Surgical Extraction against control and combo decks.

sequencing_tips: Memorize the cards Stock Up places on the bottom of your library, as they will be drawn after Doomsday Excruciator resolves.
Force the opponent to draw cards with Insatiable Avarice immediately after resolving Doomsday Excruciator to accelerate the mill win.
Prioritize discarding Doomsday Excruciator with Winternight Stories or Bitter Triumph on turns 1-2 to enable a faster combo turn.
Use Superior Spider-Man to copy Deceit early for board control, or copy Excruciator to reduce the combo to a single turn.
Common mistake: overcommitting to the board without hand resources.
Always keep interaction for the opponent's turn.
Be mindful of mana screw in U/B
Signature cards should be used to generate advantage or stabilize, not as early-game threats.
Practice stack sequencing and learn to read opponent mana states to maximize interaction efficiency.

matchups.advice: Sideboard in Cruelclaw's Heist for additional discard and to steal key combo pieces. Play for attrition and use Deceit's disruption effectively.
Use Cruelclaw's Heist to steal high-value cards. Rely on Deadly Cover-Up as Surgical Extraction and Deceit for late-game clock.
Keep maindeck Requiting Hex and Intimidation Tactics to slow the clock. Use Superior Spider-Man to copy Harvester of Misery or Deceit early.
Sideboard in Soul-Guide Lantern to shut down value generation. Use Negate for stack interaction in longer games.

Use cantrips to dig through counters, cast spells in response to their counters, or use uncounterable threats. Bait counters with cheap spells before committing your finisher. Prioritize stack control and force unfavorable trades.

Apply early pressure to disrupt land drops, use spot removal on key ramp creatures, and sideboard in additional removal or counterspells. Don't let them stabilize into their late-game. Force them to interact with your threats instead of developing their board.

Prioritize cheap interaction and board wipes. Trade efficiently, don't overdraw, and stabilize before going for the win. Sideboard in more removal and less card draw. Focus on surviving the first 3 turns and out-value them in the midgame.

Out-value them with card advantage, apply consistent pressure, and be ready to answer their sweepers. Don't overcommit to the board; maintain hand resources to respond to their control elements. Force them to spend resources on your threats rather than their game plan.

Win the resource war. Prioritize card advantage, efficient removal, and hand disruption. Control the stack, force unfavorable trades, and sideboard in targeted hate for their key pieces. The player who manages their hand and board most efficiently will win.

common_threats: Watch for landfall triggers, spell stacks, and graveyard recursion.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Dimir Excruciator is, in essence, a "Combo-Control" deck... Its strategy involves a mix of cheap disruption through removal and Deceit, combined with the interaction between Doomsday Excruciator and Restless Reef

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Excruciator exiles cards from both players' libraries, leaving each with only six cards, and Restless Reef mills four cards from the opponent's deck when it attacks
copying it with Superior Spider-Man by paying is pretty easy, giving you bounce and discard on ETB plus a 4/4 body for four mana
Another important approach involves copying Excruciator with Superior Spider-Man

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Keep in mind you can also memorize the cards it puts on the bottom, since they will be the cards you will draw after an Excruciator.
Another important mode involves forcing the opponent to draw cards after resolving Doomsday Excruciator, speeding up victory just like we do with Restless Reef.
Winternight Stories and Bitter Triumph serve as reliable discard sources to put Doomsday Excruciator into the graveyard during the early turns.
To facilitate this strategy and even execute the "combo-kill" two turns earlier... copying Excruciator with Superior Spider-Man

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: ETB effects are everywhere: from Formidable Speaker / Craterhoof Behemoth to the new Elementals
strategies running high-value cards like Jeskai Control or Elementals
already an important Standard piece against Reanimator and for shutting down value generated by Lessons decks
opponents rely on specific creatures staying on board, like Ouroboroid or Mightform Harmonizer
dealing with potential mana explosions from Badgermole Cub
